problem_statement: The paper investigates how three software organizations causally explain the risks of using generative AI in development, surfacing a self-reinforcing loop in which mistrust drives validation yet organizations can never be sure AI-generated code has been sufficiently validated.
insufficiency_evidence: "software organizations can never be certain whether AI-generated code is sufficiently validated" — Mistrust of AI, Insufficient validation of AI output, and Insufficient/insecure AI output form a perpetual circular reasoning loop
solution_mechanisms: human validation of AI output before integration
testing tools / static analysis flagging AI-introduced issues
internal usage policies for generative AI
causal mapping as a risk decision-aid for prioritization and communication
key_constructs: causal mapping of generative AI risks
notes: Unreviewed preprint; causal maps (Figures 1-4) are images and their content is only partially recoverable from the surrounding text.
